slides.title: Why backing off *makes you stronger*.
slides.sub: Your nervous system recovers slower than your muscles — and ignoring that is why your progress stalls. It's called a deload.

slides.title: More isn't always better
slides.body: Training hard every week without a break grinds down your *nervous system* faster than your muscles can recover.

slides.title: Read the warning lights
slides.body: Stalled lifts, bad sleep, low mood, lingering soreness — that's *not weakness*, it's a recovery debt.

slides.title: Plan a deload week
slides.body: Every *4 to 8 weeks*, cut your volume or intensity for one week on purpose.

slides.title: You come back stronger
slides.body: The break lets your body finish *adapting to the work* you already did — that's when the gains show up.

slides.title: Sustainable, year-round progress
slides.body: Deloading isn't lost time — it's how you train hard *for years* without burning out.

slides.title: Train hard, recover *on purpose*.
slides.body: Vibe Health scores your sleep, recovery, and training in one daily number — so you can spot when it's time to back off before you stall.

caption:
Backing off isn't lazy — it's how you actually get stronger. 💪

Your nervous system recovers slower than your muscles. Plan a deload every 4–8 weeks and let the gains catch up. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health scores your recovery so you know when to ease off → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why A Deload Week Makes You Stronger, Not Weaker
